messages.subject: [R-jobs] Statistical analyst position in Cambridge, MA
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Statistical analyst position in Cambridge, MA
messages.body_plain:
Health Insight Technologies, a VC backed startup with offices in Cambridge,
MA is looking for a motivated Statistical Analyst who is
passionate about creating statistical analyses that will provide insight
into the quality, safety and efficiency of the health care system.
Requirements for the position include:


?2-5 years direct experience working with R

?Extensive experience with data manipulation techniques in R

?Extensive experience in using R for modeling, for example, using techniques
such as supervised and unsupervised predictive analytics, including
linear/logistic regression (including lasso and other shrinkage methods),
cluster analysis, longitudinal  data and time series, random forest and
decision trees, support vector machines, and survival analysis

?Extensive experience with standard visualization methods in R, especially
the lattice package

?Experience with GIS and network analysis a plus

?Interest in extending knowledge of R to new approaches and techniques:
intense interest in exploring new techniques will be a must for this
position

?Familiarity with methods of working with large data in R

?Working knowledge of SQL preferred

?Familiarity with the medical domain is a definite plus, a passionate
interest in working with health care data is a must


Education: BS/MS in Statistics/Biostatistics or an MPH with a strong
emphasis on statistical and/or machine learning methods, or equivalent
education.

For a complete job description or to submit a cover letter/cv, please
respond to recruiting.analytics at nsight-health.com

Thank you for your interest.

messages.subject: [R-jobs] R Programming Job - Cambridge, MA
messages.body_plain:
Greetings Everyone,


My client is a cutting-edge, Clinical Informatics company with offices in
Cambridge, MA. They are looking for an R Programmer who is passionate about
using information technology to improve the quality, safety and efficiency
of the health care system. This company develops analytic systems for
physicians and hospitals that extract and present data to help improve the
safety and quality of health care.

The environment is aggressive, fast paced and innovative. They reward
motivated individuals who are dedicated to improving the quality and safety
of health care. They are looking for smart, forward-thinking employees who
will take ownership of their responsibilities, work collaboratively and
always look for ways to add to the personal and professional growth of the
company and their peers.

The ideal candidate will have strong R Programming skills and a solid
technical foundation. If you are currently working in academia and would
like to move outside the Ivy walls, this is an ideal opportunity to work for
a fast paced company with a strong mission statement and an academic ?feel?.
My client offers generous salaries with bonus and full benefits. Interested
candidates are encouraged to send their resumes to thomas at casparisgroup
dot com.

Kind regards,
Thomas Casparis

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] New SAS and R programming position with large Pharmaceutical in NJ
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] New SAS and R programming position with large Pharmaceutical in NJ
messages.body_plain:
I have an opening for a Senior Programmer in Central New Jersey with a large Pharmaceutical company.

Senior Programmer needed to create applications for automating statistical analyses and creating reports.  The candidate will generate user requirements, develop and write code, and take the lead in the validating the applications.  The primary emphasis will be on creating applications to automate the process from database through analysis and reporting for standard study designs.  
  
Minimum requirements: Five years programming experience in SAS, one year programming experience in an object-oriented language, such as C++ or R.  Experience in developing user requirements and knowledge of software testing and validation.  Excellent communication and documentation skills.  MS in statistics preferred.  Course work or training in at least one programming language other than SAS is a plus.

Ideally they want someone to:
?	Pull data from Oracle 
?	Pull info from Excel file 
?	Perform analysis in SAS, create tables 
?	From SAS call R and create graphs in R
?	Generate report with verbiage, tables, and text - report may be Word, RTF, or PDF

Remember if you are not interested, but know of someone who is, we have a 1000.00 referral fee if we place the person and they are not currently working with us. 

FOR MORE INFORMATION PLEASE CONTACT CHRISTINA KINCHEN- at CKINCHEN at SMITHHANLEY.COM or 800-684-9921 ext 80670



***********************************************************************************
Christina Kinchen
Senior Recruiter
Smith Hanley Consulting Group, LLC
an inVentiv Health Company (NASDAQ:VTIV)
accelerate results
www.smithhanleyconsulting.com

To View open positions go to: http://www.smithhanleyconsulting.com/pharma/candidates/search_jobs.asp

-Specializing in contract placement of Statisticians, SAS Programmers, Clinical Data Managers/Data Coordinators, and other clinical professionals throughout the US.

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Postdoc position in computational biology: Seattle WA
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Postdoc position in computational biology: Seattle WA
messages.body_plain:
A postdoctoral fellow position in computational biology is anticipated to 
open in the fall of 2009 at University of Washington, Seattle, WA.  The 
candidate will join a multi-disciplinary group working on edge-cutting 
research problems in computational biology.

Job Description
We seek a highly motivated and diligent researcher at the early stage of 
academic career with the ability to carry out research projects 
independently.  The position will focus on biomarker discovery for chronic 
myeloid leukemia (CML) by integrating gene expression data, resources from 
the literature and network analysis. The successful candidate will mainly 
work with Dr. Ka Yee Yeung, Ph.D. in computational biology, at University 
of Washington and Dr. Vivian Oehler, M.D. in hematology, at the Fred 
Hutchinson Cancer Research Center.  The candidate will also be part of a 
multi-disciplinary team consisting of Dr. Roger Bumgarner in the 
Department of Microbiology and Dr. Adrian Raftery in the Department of 
Statistics.  The candidate will be expected to participate in data 
analysis, algorithm and software development, to work cooperatively with 
collaborators, to actively publish and present results, and other duties 
as required.  The applicant must have a Ph.D. in biostatistics, 
statistics, computer science, computational biology or any physical 
sciences with a strong background in mathematics and computing.  The 
applicant is also expected to demonstrate excellent programming skills to 
solve complex research problems.

Location
Seattle, WA in the Pacific Northwest is widely considered to be one of the 
top US cities for young professionals.  Seattle offers residents a mild 
climate, a multitude of cultural activities, several professional sports 
teams, superb cuisine and outdoor activities including cycling, hiking, 
skiing, snowboarding, boating etc.

Application
Interested candidates should send the following materials to Dr. Ka Yee 
Yeung (kayee at u.washington.edu):
 	A cover letter highlighting your background and relationship to 
this position
 	A Curriculum Vitae (CV)
 	The names and contact details of at least three references.

The review of applications will begin immediately and will continue until 
the position is filled.  Under-represented minorities are especially 
encouraged to apply.
